rulings.Case Title: JAMES ICARD et al VS. ECOLAB, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,190
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 11, 2012, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF JAMES ICARD'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS AND FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,190.00. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Compliance With Further Responses To Document Request And Compendium Filed By Defendant Pershing Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 11, 2012, Line 18, Motion To Compel Compliance With Further Responses To Document Request And Compendium Filed By Defendant Pershing Llc - The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Watson as follows: Denied without prejudice to additional discovery. The motion would potentially require disclosures beyond the scope of the issues in the case, and, in their present form, the discovery requests at issue are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party desires a court reporter, the parties are required to meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Six Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 11, 2012, Line 18, PLAINTIFF DEEPMONEY LLC Mtn To Quash Six Subpoenas - The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Watson as follows: Granted, without prejudice to further discovery. The current text of the subpoenas is extremely broad as to subject matter, and would require production of material not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party desires a court reporter, the parties are required to meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 11, 2012, Line 5, RESPONDENT TIMOTHY MARTEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first and second causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The allegations of equitable tolling and estoppel are sufficient to preclude sustaining the demurrer on statute of limitations grounds. The first and second causes of action are adequately pled. The third cause of action fails per Stone v. Foster (1980) 106 Cal. App. 3d 334, 346-47 and it does not appear that this deficiency can be cured by amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF MANDEVILLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlewment Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 11, 2012, Line 7, PETITIONER IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF MANDEVILLA'S Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlewment Payment Rights - Due to the absence of any filings since May 14, the tentative ruling from May 14 is reinstated as follows: Denied. Mr. Pipkins is barred from transferring any of the structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock Life Insurance Company, including annuity contract 3221324, to third parties until the judgment in the federal case titled John Hancock Life Insurance Co. v. Donald C. Pipkins, action no. 3-11-CV-04992-CRB, is satisfied. Future structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock to which Mr. Pipkins is entitled may be applied to the judgment until it is satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: AMIR MORTAZAVI VS. BETTY NGUYEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 11, 2012, Line 12, PLAINTIFF AMIR MORTAZAVI'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS Denied. The motion fails for both procedural and substantive reasons. Procedurally, the motion appears to be a reprise of plaintiff's objection made to department 610 and apparently denied by that department. Thus the motion is an impermissible motion for reconsideration. Substantively, the motion is a waste of scarce judicial resources. If the court had to read this many pages every time there was a minor violation of case management rules, the court would never have time to address the many important motions made by litigants every day. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
